parameters.name: gain
parameters.override_behavior: sets pre-filter gain about 0.45 V common mode

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.override_behavior: sets each sampled low-pass update coefficient

modeling_constraints: Use deterministic rising-edge sampled gain and two-stage low-pass state updates.
Do not use current contributions, transistor-level devices, AC/noise analysis, continuous-time operators, or validation side channels.

properties.observable_contract: Initialization or active-high reset returns the preamp and both filter stages near 0.45 V and leaves settle_metric low.
At each rising clock edge, preamp_mon and metric equal gain times the sampled vin deviation about 0.45 V, clamped to 0 V through 0.9 V.
Filt1_mon applies the sampled first-order alpha update toward the bounded preamp target.
Filt2_mon applies a second sampled alpha update toward the newly updated first-stage value, and out follows filt2_mon.
After a large input change, the second-stage output visibly lags the bounded preamp target while the two stage monitors preserve cascade order.
Settle_metric is 0.9 V when the output-target error is below 0.16 V and 0.1 V while the chain is recovering.

title: Amplifier Filter Chain
